news.summary: In Pittsburgh on July 3, 2025, an inventor from Warrior, Alabama introduced a new system for commercial kitchens to combat grease buildup on hoods and decrease cleaning and maintenance needs. The innovator shared their motivation behind creating the GREASE BUSTERS, emphasizing the aim to streamline operations. The invention has the potential to significantly reduce labor costs and enhance efficiency in kitchen maintenance.
news.headline: Inventor from Warrior, Ala. creates GREASE BUSTERS to reduce grease buildup and cleaning time in commercial kitchens.

news.summary: The market for customized premixes is categorized based on various types including vitamins, minerals, amino acids, nutraceuticals, and nucleotides. These premixes find application in beverages, dairy products, cereals, bakery and confectionery items, nutrition products, dietary supplements, as well as in processed foods like soups, pasta, and ready-to-eat meals. This market segmentation allows for targeted customization to cater to specific needs and preferences in different sectors.
news.headline: Customized Premixes Market anticipated to hit $1.47B by 2030 due to rising demand for personalized nutrition. Segments include vitamins, minerals, amino acids, nutraceuticals, nucleotides, and applications span across beverages, dairy, cereals, bakery, nutrition products, dietary supplements, and processed foods, among others.

news.summary: The US stock market saw gains with the Dow Jones index rising by over 200 points on Thursday. The Dow traded at 44,725.87, up 0.54%, while the NASDAQ gained 0.61% to 20,518.19, and the S&P 500 rose by 0.51% to 6,259.36. Utilities shares jumped by 0.9%, but consumer staples stocks slipped by 0.01%. Nonfarm payrolls rose by 147,000 in June, exceeding economists' expectations of 110,000, with the unemployment rate dropping to 4.1%. Global Interactive Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: GITS) shares surged by 142% to $3.92. La Rosa Holdings Corp. (NASDAQ: LRHC) also experienced a boost.
news.headline: Dow gains over 200 points as US nonfarm payrolls beat estimates. Nonfarm payrolls rose by 147,000 in June, with a dip in unemployment rate to 4.1%.

news.summary: On Thursday, Needham upgraded Meta Platforms, Inc. (NASDAQ: META) from Underperform to Hold. Analyst Laura Martin cited improved revenue and margin expectations for 2025, driven by Meta's strong labor productivity. While the stock is trading near all-time highs, the cautious stance remains. Meta topped large-cap peers in free cash flow per employee for 2024, thanks to its globally scaled, software-centric business model. The upgrade highlights Meta's exceptional performance in this aspect. For more details, readers can find the full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Needham upgraded Meta Platforms, Inc. to Hold on improved revenue expectations for 2025. Meta ranked first in free cash flow per employee in 2024.

This initiative aims to enhance educational access and opportunities in the continent.

news.summary: The global waste oil market is estimated to be valued at USD 34.55 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 47.01 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 4.5% from 2025 to 2032. The market growth is driven by environmental awareness, sustainable waste management solutions, and stringent regulations promoting waste oil recycling and reuse. By 2025, the used engine oil segment is expected to reach about USD 12.64 billion, with recycling and re-refining applications holding a market share of 35.7%. In terms of treatment methods, landfilling is predicted to account for over two-fifths of the global waste oil market share in 2025. North America is a significant market, with a share of 38.7% in 2025, while Asia Pacific is expected to exhibit the fastest growth due to rapid urbanization and industrialization. The industry faces challenges in high processing and recycling costs hindering market expansion, despite rising demand for recycled and refined waste oil products. Regulatory support for sustainable practices is creating growth opportunities in the market.
news.headline: Global Waste Oil Market expected to reach USD 47.01 Bn by 2032 with a 4.5% CAGR growth fueled by environmental awareness and regulations. North America leading with 38.7% share in 2025. Landfilling segment to account for over two-fifths market share in 2025. High processing costs limit market expansion. news.summary: Dublin-based ResearchAndMarkets.com has recently released a report titled "Agricultural Surfactants - A Global Market Overview," which is now available for access. The global market size for agricultural surfactants currently stands at US$1.8 billion for 2024 and is projected to climb to US$2.6 billion by 2030, indicating a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 6.5% during the forecast period spanning from 2024 to 2030. The surge in the agricultural surfactants market is attributed to the escalating demand for improved crop protection and heightened agricultural output resulting from various factors.
news.headline: Global agricultural surfactants market valued at $1.8 billion in 2024, expected to reach $2.6 billion by 2030 with a CAGR of 6.5%.

news.summary: In July 3rd, 2025, economic data from TEMPE, Arizona showed positive growth in the services sector. The Business Activity Index stood at 54.2%, indicating expansion. New Orders Index followed at 51.3%, reflecting increased demand. However, there was a slight decline in the Employment Index at 47.2%, highlighting potential job market challenges. Supplier Deliveries Index was at 50.3%, indicating stable delivery times. This growth in economic activity came after a brief contraction the previous month.
news.headline: Services PMI® for June 2025 is at 50.8%, with Business Activity Index at 54.2% and Employment Index at 47.2%.

news.summary: In BROOMFIELD, Colorado, on July 3, 2025, the Air Force Research Laboratory (AFRL) chose BAE Systems (LON: BA) to further progress and aid in the deployment of the Battlefield Assisted Trauma Distributed Operations Kit (BATDOK®). This partnership aims to enhance medical support on the battlefield. The project focuses on optimizing the forces and aims to improve the handling of trauma cases.
news.headline: BAE Systems chosen by AFRL to develop AI software for battlefield medical care, including BATDOK® kit.

The media company Assembly, a key player in the Stagwell challenger network, unveiled Assembly Control on July 3, 2025. news.summary: The global Healthcare Distribution Market is projected to reach a value of USD 1,120.67 billion in 2025, with an expected growth to USD 1,765.68 billion by 2032, showcasing a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 6.7% during this period. Factors such as rising healthcare costs, an increase in chronic and lifestyle-related diseases, the rise of online distribution channels, and the expansion of medical infrastructure are fueling the demand for pharmaceutical and medical device distribution services globally. The pharmaceutical segment is anticipated to dominate the market share in 2025, with hospital pharmacies being a significant end user segment. North America is likely to lead with over two-fifths of the market share in 2025, while Asia Pacific is expected to emerge as a profitable market. The growing demand for pharmaceuticals and medical devices due to the surge in chronic diseases is a key driver for market growth. However, regulatory hurdles and supply chain disruptions may hinder the market's progress, particularly due to stringent regulations from bodies like the FDA and EMA, and global supply chain disturbances caused by geopolitical conflicts, pandemics, and natural disasters.
news.headline: Global Healthcare Distribution Market to reach USD 1,765.68 Bn by 2032, with a CAGR of 6.7% from 2025 to 2032. North America dominates market with over two-fifths share in 2025. Rising demand for pharmaceuticals and medical devices boosts market growth. Regulatory hurdles and supply chain disruptions may constrain growth.

news.summary: In a significant move, the Texas Legislature is propelling Texas to the forefront of next-generation nuclear technology, water security, and medical research on a national and global scale. This initiative, based in ABILENE, Texas, is a collaborative effort between Texas Tech University, Abilene Christian University, and Natura Resources. These institutions are at the forefront of this advancement, underscoring Texas's commitment to innovation. This endeavor marks a pivotal moment as Texas aims to solidify its position as a leader in these critical sectors.
news.headline: Texas Tech, ACU, and Natura Resources collaborate on nuclear reactor, water desalination advancements in Texas.

news.summary: On July 3, 2025, in Pittsburgh, an inventor from Little Rock, Arkansas, shared a new innovation. The inventor created a cap device designed for ground-based sprinkler systems to safeguard against unwanted trash and debris infiltrating the backflow valve interface openings. The invention, known as THE BACKFLOW CAP, addresses the need to prevent such interference, offering a solution to enhance the efficiency of these systems. This invention aims to mitigate potential issues caused by debris, ensuring the optimal functioning of ground-based sprinkler systems.
news.headline: InventHelp inventor from Little Rock develops THE BACKFLOW CAP to prevent debris from entering sprinkler valve interfaces.

news.summary: In Ottawa, Ontario, striking Canadian Hearing Services (CHS) employees from CUPE 2073 will be conducting an informational picket outside Accreditation Canada’s office today. The strike, now in its tenth week, has left Deaf clients feeling neglected and isolated without vital services. Amidst reports of CHS showing no urgency to resolve the situation, workers are beginning to question the organization’s accredited status. Following the appointment of CEO Julia Dumanian, concerns have been raised about her medicalized approach to management.
news.headline: Canadian Hearing Services workers are striking for 10 weeks, picketing outside Accreditation Canada office to protest lack of services. CEO under scrutiny.
